title: Dart Basics Mock Test
description: Test your understanding of Dart fundamentals including variables, data types, control flow, functions, and basic OOP concepts

questions.text: What is the entry point of every Dart program?
questions.explanation: The main() function is the entry point of every Dart application. Execution begins here.

questions.text: Which of the following is a valid variable declaration in Dart?
questions.explanation: Variable names must start with a letter or underscore, not numbers or special characters. 'int number = 10;' follows Dart naming conventions.

questions.text: What will be the output of this code? `print(5 ~/ 2);`
questions.explanation: The '~/' operator performs integer division, returning the quotient as an integer. 5 ~/ 2 = 2 (discards remainder).

questions.text: Which keyword is used to define a constant value that must be known at compile time?
questions.explanation: const creates compile-time constants that must be known at compile time. final creates runtime constants that can be set once.

questions.text: What is the correct way to declare a function that returns a String in Dart?
questions.explanation: Functions in Dart specify the return type before the function name. 'String getName() { return 'John'; }' is correct syntax.

questions.text: What does the '??' operator do in Dart?
options: Null-aware coalescing operator - returns left if not null, otherwise right
questions.explanation: The '??' operator returns the left expression if it's not null, otherwise returns the right expression. Example: `name ?? 'Guest'`

questions.text: How do you create a List with fixed length in Dart?
questions.explanation: List.filled(5, 0) creates a fixed-length list of 5 elements, all initialized to 0. Regular lists are growable by default.
Strings can be accessed like lists using index notation. Index 0 = 'D', 1 = 'a', 2 = 'r', so output is 'r'.

questions.text: Which loop is guaranteed to execute at least once?
questions.explanation: do-while loop executes the code block first, then checks the condition, guaranteeing at least one execution regardless of the condition.

questions.text: What is the correct way to create a class with a constructor in Dart?
questions.explanation: Constructors have the same name as the class. 'Person(String name) { }' is the correct constructor syntax in Dart.

questions.text: What does the 'assert' statement do in Dart?
options: Checks a condition and throws an error if false (only in development)
questions.explanation: assert checks a condition and throws an AssertionError if false. It only works in development mode and is ignored in production.

questions.text: How do you import a custom Dart file named 'math_utils.dart'?
questions.explanation: The 'import' keyword is used to bring external libraries or files into the current Dart file. Example: import 'math_utils.dart';
